records.ety: [OF. peier, fr. L. picare to pitch, pix pitch: cf. OF. peiz pitch, F. poix. See Pitch a black substance.]

defs: To cover, as bottom of a vessel, a seam, a spar, etc., with tar or pitch, or waterproof composition of tallow, resin, etc.; to smear.

records.ety: [OE. paien, F. payer, fr. L. pacare to pacify, appease, fr. pax, pacis, peace. See Peace.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Paid (pād); p. pr. & vb. n. Paying.]

defs: To satisfy, or content; specifically, to satisfy (another person) for service rendered, property delivered, etc.; to discharge one's obligation to; to make due return to; to compensate; to remunerate; to recompense; to requite; as, to pay workmen or servants.
quotes: May no penny ale them pay [i. e., satisfy].

defs: Hence, figuratively: To compensate justly; to requite according to merit; to reward; to punish; to retort or retaliate upon.
quotes: For which, or pay me quickly, or I'll pay you.

defs: To discharge, as a debt, demand, or obligation, by giving or doing what is due or required; to deliver the amount or value of to the person to whom it is owing; to discharge a debt by delivering (money owed).
quotes: Have patience with me, and I will pay thee all.
If they pay this tax, they starve.

defs: To discharge or fulfill, as a duty; to perform or render duty, as that which has been promised.
quotes: This day have I paid my vows.

defs: To give or offer, without an implied obligation; as, to pay attention; to pay a visit.

Hence, to make or secure suitable return for expense or trouble; to be remunerative or profitable; to be worth the effort or pains required; as, it will pay to ride; it will pay to wait; politeness always pays.
quotes: 'T was I paid for your sleeps; I watched your wakings.

defs: An equivalent or return for money due, goods purchased, or services performed; salary or wages for work or service; compensation; recompense; payment; hire; as, the pay of a clerk; the pay of a soldier.
quotes: Where only merit constant pay receives.
There is neither pay nor plunder to be got.
